name: stage2-rvv-low-precision-contraction-primitive-surface-gate1
title: Stage2 RVV low-precision contraction primitive surface campaign
description: Macro campaign for RVV low-precision contraction primitives. Gates 1-4 are complete under the packed-i4 campaign-level no-further-repair admission closure: route support and correctness fallback remain allowed, while performance-preferred dispatch is denied until a future typed/provider repair plus source-backed measured-win evidence updates provider, target, evidence-root, and policy facts together.
creator: codex
notes: Gates 1-3 are complete. Gate 4 is complete under the campaign-level no-further-repair admission closure. The packed-i4 dequant/dequant-clamp production route keeps the provider-owned high-nibble vwmacc product loop plus scalar dequant/dequant-clamp epilogue, with loop-body steps 11, peak live vector groups 5/32, source-backed dequant no-win range 0.895307..1.027027, and source-backed dequant-clamp no-win range 0.874735..1.061579. The current provider/resource admission tuple is contract rvv-low-precision-packed-i4-campaign-no-further-repair-admission.v1, performance decision deny-performance-preferred-with-campaign-no-further-repair-no-win-blocker, performance closure no-further-repair-packed-i4-campaign-loop-11-budget-5of32.v1, beyond-local decision deny-performance-preferred-campaign-no-further-provider-repair, blocker packed-i4-campaign-no-further-provider-repair-after-scalar-epilogue-no-win, maturity evidence same-target-packed-i4-campaign-no-further-repair-no-win-gate4.v1, and reopen requirement new-typed-provider-campaign-repair-plus-source-backed-measured-win-and-updated-admission-facts.v1. This closure is consumed through Gearbox resource facts, selected-body realization and gearbox_cross_region_handoff, route-family and statement-plan validation, route metadata, target artifact/header mirrors, generated-bundle scripts, same-target measurement records/evidence roots, production-pressure profiles, and RVVLowPrecisionPerformancePolicy. No fresh ssh rvv timing was required in the final closure slice because only admission/policy facts and mirrors changed; generated runtime statements, resource counts, and performance claims did not change. Future packed-i4 performance-preferred dispatch may reopen only with a new typed/provider-owned repair plus source-backed measured-win evidence for the same target and updated provider, target, evidence-root, closure/reopen, and policy facts. Metadata, artifact names, route ids, generated-bundle status, report text, or q8/q4 labels are not authority.
